event: History of surgery for appendicitis
History of 5 tattoos without complications
Mastopexy with placement of 240-mL silicone implants
Discharged from hospital
Ulceration and necrosis at the incision
Hemoglobin 7.5 g/dL, hematocrit 23%, leukocytes 64,500/mm, C-reactive protein >270 mg/L, low levels of total proteins and fractions, serum albumin, and globulins
Transferred to the intensive care unit with suspected infection of the surgical site and sepsis
Received systemic vancomycin and meronem for 20 days and daptomycin (Cubicin)
Local wound care with saline solution and chlorhexidine antiseptic
Ulceration and necrosis worsened
Surgical removal of the silicone implants
Rinsed with 2 L of saline, garamycin 80 mg, and kefazol 1 g
Breast reconstruction using mononylon sutures
Biopsies of the edges of the surgical wound showed dermatitis and diffuse neutrophilic panniculitis and necrosis
New dehiscence of the entire surgical wound
Large area of necrosis with no secretion
Wound care using negative pressure VAC and hydrophobic polyurethane foam sponges with silver
Notable purplish secretion aspirated by the VAC machine
Worsening of the patient's clinical condition and anasarca
Introduced prednisone 40 mg/d
Improved general condition, granulation tissue, contraction of the wound edges, and some adherence between the structures of the pectoralis major muscle and the glandular tissue
Began to use hydrophobic polyvinyl alcohol (white) foam in the next 3 VAC foam changes
Placed deep mononylon sutures to direct tissue adhesion without transfixing the dermis or the epidermis
Improved appearance of the wound after 4 foam changes
Performed dressing changes using sodium carboxymethyl cellulose with silver (Aquacel) outside the operating room
Discharged 42 days after the original surgery with prednisone 60 mg/d
Wound care conducted in the outpatient clinic with progressive improvement in the wound's appearance
Gradually reduced and eventually suspended prednisone after 4 months
Initial sensitivity in the areola and nipple
